_meta.source: 4 Sonnet proposer chunks, Haiku fresh-verified, adjudicated per ADJUDICATION-s213.md

candidates.quote: When he laid the edge of the blade against the swollen throat of the creature on the straw, the skin split open in a gout of black blood and yellow pus.
candidates.note: Reek (Theon) mercy-kills the dying Ralf Kenning with Kenning's own sword.

candidates.quote: Ralf Kenning has the command.
candidates.note: ironborn side; nominal garrison commander, incapacitated/dying by the time Reek arrives.

candidates.quote: He killed them both, if you can believe that.
candidates.note: Dagon Codd kills the two starving cannibals found in the Children's Tower (guard's report to Reek).

candidates.quote: It was the one-armed man who’d flung the axe.
candidates.note: Adrack Humble (identified two paragraphs later as the one-armed man) kills Dagon Codd with a thrown axe.

candidates.quote: I am here at the command of Ramsay Bolton, Lord of the Hornwood and heir to the Dreadfort, who captured me at Winterfell.
candidates.note: Bolton/northern side; Ramsay commands the northern host besieging Moat Cailin and dispatches Reek to negotiate surrender.

candidates.quote: Victarion commanded us to hold, he did.
candidates.note: ironborn side; Victarion's standing order to the garrison, though he had already departed for the kingsmoot before the fall.

candidates.quote: He smashed the Iron Fleet off Fair Isle.
candidates.note: royal side; overlaps with existing DEFEATS(Decisive royal victory) track_b result — role edge reifies the hub commander.

candidates.quote: Stannis Baratheon had descended on the Iron Fleet from both north and south whilst they were trapped in the channel between the island and the mainland, dealing Victarion his most crushing defeat.
candidates.note: ironborn side; Victarion commanded the Iron Fleet and suffered the defeat.

candidates.quote: In the end the Golden Storm went down off Fair Isle during Balon’s first rebellion, cut in half by a towering war galley called Fury when Stannis Baratheon caught Victarion in his trap and smashed the Iron Fleet.
candidates.note: Aeron's own ship, Golden Storm, is sunk beneath him; he is then fished out and taken captive (harm subtype: battle/capture).

candidates.quote: Lord Randyll had decided matters before Tyrell ever found the battlefield.
candidates.note: loyalist/Reach side; Randyll's van, not Mace Tyrell's main host, actually won the battle.

candidates.quote: Lord Cafferen died at Ashford Castle, cut down by Randyll Tarly whilst fighting for Robert.
candidates.note: personally kills Lord Cafferen; distinct textual beat from the command-tier role (R1-10).

candidates.quote: Lord Cafferen died at Ashford Castle, cut down by Randyll Tarly whilst fighting for Robert.
candidates.note: fighting for Robert's rebel side; killed by Randyll Tarly.

candidates.quote: He defeated my brother once, at Ashford.
candidates.note: rebel side, defeated; Stannis speaking of Robert.

candidates.quote: Lord Alyn had taken his ships south, not north.
candidates.note: Velaryon/Iron Throne side; Alyn disregards Gedmund Peake's orders and independently commands the Velaryon squadron into the attack.

candidates.quote: The Grand Defiance, a towering Braavosi dromond of four hundred oars, fought her way past half a dozen smaller Velaryon warships to gain the open sea, only to find Lord Alyn himself bearing down on her.
candidates.note: personally engages and sinks the Grand Defiance aboard his flagship Queen Rhaenys; distinct beat from the command-decision role (R1-14).

candidates.quote: he was honored in a lavish ceremony in the Red Keep, with King Aegon III seated on the Iron Throne and the court and half the city looking on.
candidates.note: the victory-ceremony/knighting is narrated as part of this node's Aftermath, not a separately-sluged event; honoree of the ceremony.

candidates.quote: He lost three ships in the Stepstones (one, sadly, was the True Heart, captained by his cousin Daeron, who perished when she sank)
candidates.note: dies when his ship True Heart is sunk during the attack.

candidates.quote: Ser Marston Waters, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, dubbed him a knight.
candidates.note: performs the ceremonial knighting at the victory ceremony bundled into this node's Aftermath.

candidates.quote: Unwin Peake, Lord Regent and Hand of the King, draped an admiral’s golden chain about his neck and presented him with a silver replica of the Queen Rhaenys as a token of his victory.
candidates.note: conferrer role at the HONORED_AT ceremony (R1-16), per rule the conferrer takes AGENT_IN, not COMMANDS_IN — Peake did NOT order this specific unauthorized attack (see flags).

candidates.quote: the King’s Hand dispatched fellow regent Lord Manfryd Mooton to Braavos to treat with the Sealord and return his elephant.
candidates.note: non-combat diplomatic role resolving the attack's fallout with Braavos.

candidates.quote: Father commanded me to take Deepwood Motte,
candidates.note: ironborn side; orders Asha to take Deepwood Motte (Asha reporting his command).

candidates.quote: You needed a thousand and a moon’s turn to take Deepwood Motte.
candidates.note: ironborn side; leads the thousand-man expedition that takes the castle (Theon needling her about it).

candidates.quote: Glover’s steward had to be carried up from the cellar, having lost a leg when Asha took the castle.
candidates.note: wounded (lost a leg) during Asha's assault; see flags re: this node's siege-vs-battle framing.

candidates.quote: we knew that Jon Arryn had taken Gulltown, though.
candidates.note: rebel side; leads the Vale bannermen against Gulltown.

candidates.quote: Robert was the first man to gain the wall, and slew Marq Grafton with his own hand.
candidates.note: personally first over the wall and personally kills Marq Grafton.

candidates.quote: Robert was the first man to gain the wall, and slew Marq Grafton with his own hand.
candidates.note: loyalist/House Grafton side; killed by Robert.

candidates.quote: He had won his spurs during Robert’s Rebellion, she knew, fighting first against Lord Jon Arryn at the gates of Gulltown
candidates.note: loyalist/House Grafton side at the time of this battle (switched to Arryn's service only afterward, per wiki Aftermath — text doesn't show the switch on-page).

candidates.quote: He recited his vows in a high, childish voice, promising his love and devotion to Mace Tyrell’s twice-widowed daughter.
candidates.note: groom personally speaking his vows; on-page ceremony via Cersei's POV.

candidates.quote: the bride sank gracefully to her knees and Tommen covered her with the heavy cloth-of-gold monstrosity that Robert had cloaked Cersei in on their own wedding day, with the crowned stag of Baratheon worked upon its back in beads of onyx.
candidates.note: the bride; a principal of the ceremony (changing of the cloaks), not an audience member — no cleaner single vocab slot for 'bride' than PARTICIPATES_IN.

candidates.quote: “Yes.” Cersei beckoned to Jaime. “Lord Commander, escort His Grace and his little queen to their pillows, if you would.”
candidates.note: tasked at the feast to escort the king and queen to the bedding; accepts and performs it — logistical/supportive, not guest-only

candidates.quote: They will have him stumbling and shuffling like a fool by the time they’re done, Cersei thought resentfully as she watched.
candidates.note: present at the feast as regent/mother of the groom, watching the dancing; organizer role belongs to Lady Alerie per the same chapter

candidates.quote: the Queen of Thorns had balked her in that as well. “That old thing?” the crone had said.
candidates.note: overrides Cersei on the wedding attire — organizational involvement in staging the ceremony.

candidates.quote: Margaery took a turn around the floor with her father, then another with her brother Loras.
candidates.note: guest/dancer at the wedding feast.

candidates.quote: Margaery took a turn around the floor with her father, then another with her brother Loras.
candidates.note: guest/dancer at the wedding feast (bride's father).

candidates.quote: The other cousin, Elinor, was sharing a cup of wine with the handsome young Bastard of Driftmark, Aurane Waters.
candidates.note: guest at the wedding feast.

candidates.quote: Roose Bolton’s bastard had started it by seizing Lady Hornwood as she returned from the harvest feast, marrying her that very night even though he was young enough to be her son.
candidates.note: starts the conflict by seizing/forcibly marrying Lady Hornwood.

candidates.quote: He won’t ever be my lord! He made Lady Hornwood marry him, then shut her in a dungeon and made her eat her fingers.
candidates.note: victim of Ramsay's abduction/forced marriage/imprisonment/maiming (harm-gate: node_type event.battle, subject matter is abduction+maiming).

candidates.quote: Then Lord Manderly had taken her castle.
candidates.note: Manderly side; seizes Hornwood castle from the Boltons in the contest.

candidates.quote: Redwyne was encamped beneath the walls of Dragonstone, however, and the greater part of his fleet was engaged in ferrying men across Blackwater Bay for the assault on that island stronghold.
candidates.note: Iron Throne/greens side; original besieging commander before Loras takes over.

candidates.quote: “Splendid. Let Sweet Cersei carry our Knight of Flowers to Dragonstone at once. Ser Loras, the command is yours. Swear to me that you shall not return until Dragonstone is Tommen’s.”
candidates.note: Iron Throne/greens side; Cersei grants Loras command in place of Redwyne.

candidates.quote: Aye, she thought, but not so dark as in the Maidenvault, or on Dragonstone where Loras Tyrell lies burned and bleeding, or down in the black cells beneath the castle.
candidates.note: gravely wounded in the assault (harm-gate: node_type event.battle, subtype wounding).

candidates.quote: “All the fighting men,” Ser Brynden replied. “A few camp followers and serving men were left with their wounded. We questioned as many as we needed, to be certain of the truth. They started leaving at nightfall, stealing off in ones and twos at first, and then in larger groups. The wounded men and servants were told to keep the campfires lit so no one would know they’d gone, but once the rains began it didn’t matter.”
candidates.note: the Karstark host (collective) is the agent of its own desertion; house-node used since the deserting riders are never individually named. No VICTIM_IN per S212 lesson (event.incident is not a harm subtype).

candidates.quote: At their head rode the Lord of Barrowton, Roderick Dustin, a warrior so old and hoary men called
candidates.note: blacks side; leads the Winter Wolves northmen contingent.

candidates.quote: Rivers, known as the Bowman of Raventree. The northmen numbered two thousand, Frey commanded two hundred knights and thrice as many foot, Rivers brought three hundred archers to the fray.
candidates.note: blacks side; explicitly stated to command his knights/foot contingent.

candidates.quote: By nightfall two thousand men were dead, amongst them many notables, including Lord Frey, Lord Lefford, Lord Bigglestone, Lord Charlton, Lord Swyft, Lord Reyne, Ser Clarent Crakehall, and Ser Emory Hill, the Bastard of Lannisport.
candidates.note: killed in the battle (harm-gate: node_type event.battle).

candidates.quote: Rivers, known as the Bowman of Raventree. The northmen numbered two thousand, Frey commanded two hundred knights and thrice as many foot, Rivers brought three hundred archers to the fray.
candidates.note: blacks side; brought and led the archer contingent (also personally shot down Aemond's ravens per surrounding text).

candidates.quote: it was under the aged Lord Humfrey Lefford, who had suffered so many wounds that he commanded
candidates.note: greens/Lannister side commander.

candidates.quote: By nightfall two thousand men were dead, amongst them many notables, including Lord Frey, Lord Lefford, Lord Bigglestone, Lord Charlton, Lord Swyft, Lord Reyne, Ser Clarent Crakehall, and Ser Emory Hill, the Bastard of Lannisport.
candidates.note: killed in the battle (harm-gate: node_type event.battle).

candidates.quote: Raventree, the eleven-year-old Benjicot Blackwood. With their numbers augmented by these fresh levies, the queen's men agreed that the time had come to attack.
candidates.note: blacks side; boy Lord of Raventree, nominal commander of reinforcement levies (also wept seeing the dead afterward).

candidates.quote: More rivermen turned up the next day, led by Ser Garibald Grey, Lord Jon Charlton, and the new Lord of
candidates.note: blacks side; leads reinforcement rivermen the day before the battle.

candidates.quote: Broome drove a spear into his huge pale belly.
candidates.note: personally kills castellan Robert Quince during the coup; also traduced the garrison and led the men who burst in on Quince.

candidates.quote: As Quince struggled to rise from his bed, Broome drove a spear into his huge pale belly.
candidates.note: castellan killed in the coup (harm-gate: node_type event.battle).

candidates.quote: When Alfred Broome drew his sword to slay her, Marston Waters wrenched the blade from his hand.
candidates.note: prevents Broome from killing the captured Baela; also the one who surprised Gerardys in the rookery earlier in the same coup.

candidates.quote: Ser Marston surprised Grand Maester Gerardys in his rookery, so no word of the attack might escape by raven.
candidates.note: captured during the coup (harm-gate subtype: capture). His later execution is a separate node (execution-of-grand-maester-gerardys), not covered here.

candidates.quote: As Tom Tangletongue and his ruffians smashed down the door of Lady Baela’s bedchamber to take her prisoner, the girl slipped out her window, scrambling across rooftops and down walls until she reached the yard.
candidates.note: leads the attempt to seize Baela during the coup.

candidates.quote: up to meet him rose Baela Targaryen, Prince Daemon's daughter by the Lady
candidates.note: mounts Moondancer and fights Aegon/Sunfyre in the air over the castle.

candidates.quote: So it came to pass that when King Aegon II flew Sunfyre over Dragonmont's smoking peak and made his descent
candidates.note: personally fights the aerial duel against Baela/Moondancer astride Sunfyre; also shatters both legs leaping clear of the crash.

candidates.quote: The green dragon soon lay still.
candidates.note: killed in the aerial duel against Sunfyre (harm-gate: node_type event.battle).

candidates.quote: Ser Criston Cole abandoned Harrenhal, striking south along the western shore of the Gods Eye, with thirty-six-hundred men behind him
candidates.note: greens commander leading the host south from Harrenhal toward the ridge fight

candidates.quote: up on the ridge Red Robb Rivers and two of his archers raised their longbows. Three arrows flew across the field, striking Cole in belly, neck, and breast.
candidates.note: fires the killing shots on Criston Cole at Longleaf's instigation

candidates.quote: Three arrows flew across the field, striking Cole in belly, neck, and breast.
candidates.note: killed by Red Robb Rivers's arrows before the pitched battle even began; harm-gate ok (event.battle)

candidates.quote: But Longleaf the Lionslayer said, “I'll want three more,” and up on the ridge Red Robb Rivers and two of his archers raised their longbows.
candidates.note: blacks side, orders the archers to kill Criston rather than duel him personally — instigator, did not personally execute

candidates.quote: Lord Roderick raised a warhorn to his lips and sounded the charge, and the queen's men came screaming down the ridge
candidates.note: blacks side, sounds the charge that routs the greens

candidates.quote: Chief amongst them was Ser Garibald Grey in his dented plate and mail.
candidates.note: one of the three blacks Criston challenged to single/group combat before Longleaf called for archers instead; present combatant, later credited with coining “Butcher's Ball”

candidates.quote: while the Greatjon had seized the gold mines at Castamere, Nunn’s Deep, and the Pendric Hills.
candidates.note: the Greatjon personally executes the mine seizures during Robb's post-Oxcross westerlands campaign

candidates.quote: Since the battle, he sees no one, but broods in his Stone Drum.
candidates.note: the retreat itself happens off-page between ACOK and ASOS; Salladhor Saan's on-page report (secondhand => tier-2) confirms Stannis withdrew to Dragonstone

candidates.quote: only those knights that we took off at the end, and no ships but my little brave few
candidates.note: his Lysene ships evacuated Stannis's surviving men from the Blackwater — logistical/non-combat support role in the retreat

candidates.quote: They come round sniffing after you, my lord, but we saw them off, and buried three down by the turnips.
candidates.note: Sow's Horn garrison commander whose men repel and kill the Karstark raiders

candidates.quote: Every castle in Dorne, save Sunspear, was burned thrice over, as Balerion and Vhagar returned time and time again.
candidates.note: personally flies Balerion in the punitive two-year burning campaign

candidates.quote: Every castle in Dorne, save Sunspear, was burned thrice over, as Balerion and Vhagar returned time and time again.
candidates.note: personally flies Vhagar in the same burning campaign

candidates.quote: Queen Visenya was set upon one night in King’s Landing.
candidates.note: target of a Dornish assassination attempt during the Wroth period (she survived and cut the last attacker down herself — distinct AGENT_IN act not separately minted to avoid a redundant same-type role); harm-gate ok (event.battle)

candidates.quote: It was to them that Prince Jacaerys turned, at the urging of his fool, vowing that any man who could master a dragon would be granted lands and riches and dubbed a knight.
candidates.note: instigator/orderer of the Sowing — issues the call, does not personally attempt to mount a dragon

candidates.quote: Vermithor, the Old King’s own dragon, bent his neck to a blacksmith’s bastard, a towering man called Hugh the Hammer or Hard Hugh
candidates.note: personally mounts/tames Vermithor during the Sowing

candidates.quote: whilst a palehaired man-atarms named Ulf the White (for his hair) or Ulf the Sot (for his drinking) mounted Silverwing, beloved of Good Queen Alysanne.
candidates.note: personally mounts/tames Silverwing during the Sowing; “man-atarms” is a verbatim OCR artifact, not a typo

candidates.quote: And when Addam of Hull mounted Ser Laenor's dragon, Seasmoke, it seemed to prove the truth of his mother's claims.
candidates.note: personally mounts/tames Seasmoke during the Sowing

candidates.quote: Prince Aegon commanded a charge, hoping to break through the Kingslanders before the other loyalists fell upon his flanks, and mounted Quicksilver to lead the attack himself.
candidates.note: pretender/rebel side; personally led the cavalry charge at the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye (PART_OF this war)

candidates.quote: Black Dread had appeared in the southern sky. King Maegor had come.
candidates.note: loyalist/king's side; arrives personally on Balerion and turns the battle by fighting — field command belonged to Davos Darklyn

candidates.quote: Ser Davos Darklyn of the Kingsguard marshalled five thousand swords in King’s Landing and struck out west to meet the rebels.
candidates.note: loyalist side field commander; later slain in the battle by Lord Corbray

candidates.quote: The Piper levies were led not by Lord Jon or his brothers, but by their sister Melony, who donned man's mail and took up a spear.
candidates.note: rebel/pretender side; personally led House Piper's levies, died in the battle

candidates.quote: five hundred men under the banner of a bastard son of Lyman Lannister, Ser Tyler Hill, by which ploy the cunning Lord of Casterly Rock lent supporters to the young prince whilst still keeping his own hands clean, should Maegor prevail.
candidates.note: rebel/pretender side; commanded the 500 men covertly banked by Lyman Lannister

candidates.quote: five hundred men under the banner of a bastard son of Lyman Lannister, Ser Tyler Hill, by which ploy the cunning Lord of Casterly Rock lent supporters to the young prince whilst still keeping his own hands clean, should Maegor prevail.
candidates.note: covert non-combat logistical support to the rebel side via his bastard son Tyler Hill; never openly declared for either side

candidates.quote: Lady Jeyne of House Westerling had been married to Alyn Tarbeck, who had died with Prince Aegon in the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye.
candidates.note: rebel side; died fighting at the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye (PART_OF this war)

candidates.quote: A bright bud of blood blossomed where his sword pressed into Mycah’s flesh, and a slow red line trickled down the boy’s cheek.
candidates.note: cut Mycah with Lion's Tooth, provoking the confrontation

candidates.quote: Arya swung with both hands. There was a loud crack as the wood split against the back of the prince’s head
candidates.note: struck Joffrey with her stick, then threw his sword Lion's Tooth into the river

candidates.quote: Then a grey blur flashed past her, and suddenly Nymeria was there, leaping, jaws closing around Joffrey’s sword arm.
candidates.note: bit Joffrey's sword arm, knocking the sword from his hand

candidates.quote: She watched helplessly, almost blind from her tears.
candidates.note: present throughout, directly perceives the whole confrontation (text-anchor gate met: she watches, not shielded)

candidates.quote: the heavy cloth-of-gold monstrosity that Robert had cloaked Cersei in on their own wedding day, with the crowned stag of Baratheon worked upon its back in beads of onyx
candidates.note: performed the changing-of-the-cloaks ritual on Cersei at their own wedding; recalled via the Tommen/Margaery wedding scene

candidates.quote: The night of our wedding feast, the first time we shared a bed, he called me by your sister’s name.
candidates.note: Cersei's own account of her wedding night as Robert's bride

candidates.quote: Served in the honor guard that brought his sister the Lady Cersei Lannister to King’s Landing to wed King Robert.
candidates.note: White Book entry; non-combat escort duty bringing the bride to the capital

candidates.quote: Champion in the tourney held at King’s Landing on the occasion of their wedding.
candidates.note: White Book entry; won the wedding tourney (node typed event.tournament, covers both wedding and its tourney)

candidates.quote: Gerion gave him a gilded dagger with an ivory grip and a sapphire pommel for a wedding gift
candidates.note: gave the king a wedding gift

candidates.quote: At Summerhall he won three battles in a single day, and brought Lords Grandison and Cafferen back to Storm’s End as prisoners.
candidates.note: rebel side; Stannis recounting Robert's command in the three battles

candidates.quote: He slew Lord Fell in single combat and captured his son Silveraxe.
candidates.note: personally killed Lord Fell and captured Silveraxe, distinct from the overall command role

candidates.quote: He slew Lord Fell in single combat and captured his son Silveraxe.
candidates.note: killed by Robert in single combat; harm-gate satisfied (event.battle)

candidates.quote: At Summerhall he won three battles in a single day, and brought Lords Grandison and Cafferen back to Storm’s End as prisoners.
candidates.note: captured at Summerhall; harm-gate satisfied (capture)

candidates.quote: At Summerhall he won three battles in a single day, and brought Lords Grandison and Cafferen back to Storm’s End as prisoners.
candidates.note: captured at Summerhall; harm-gate satisfied (capture); later dies at the separate Battle of Ashford

candidates.quote: He slew Lord Fell in single combat and captured his son Silveraxe.
candidates.note: captured at Summerhall alongside his father's death; harm-gate satisfied (capture)

candidates.quote: At Robert’s coronation, I was made to kneel at the royal feet beside Grand Maester Pycelle and Varys the eunuch, so that he might forgive us our crimes before he took us into his service.
candidates.note: as king, personally granted the pardons to Jaime/Pycelle/Varys at his own coronation

candidates.quote: At Robert’s coronation, I was made to kneel at the royal feet beside Grand Maester Pycelle and Varys the eunuch, so that he might forgive us our crimes before he took us into his service.
candidates.note: knelt to receive royal pardon at the coronation ceremony

candidates.quote: At Robert’s coronation, I was made to kneel at the royal feet beside Grand Maester Pycelle and Varys the eunuch, so that he might forgive us our crimes before he took us into his service.
candidates.note: knelt to receive royal pardon at the coronation ceremony

candidates.quote: At Robert’s coronation, I was made to kneel at the royal feet beside Grand Maester Pycelle and Varys the eunuch, so that he might forgive us our crimes before he took us into his service.
candidates.note: knelt to receive royal pardon at the coronation ceremony
